summary: We are pivoting hard toward Asia—prioritizing the Intelligent China and Asia Star programs, launching a joint CKGSB-IE 'New Chinese Playbook for Going Global' report in Singapore this September, and testing AI vs. traditional campaign strategies—while the knowledge website redesign and social media agency selection demand immediate attention this week.
summaryDetail: The biggest initiative is the 'New Chinese Playbook for Going Global' report with IE, targeting a September launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson events in Singapore—Felix needs to finalize the one-pager proposal and secure event partnerships this week. Meanwhile, the team is prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships, with a deliberate A/B test running for Asia Start: Joseph uses AI, Daisy uses traditional tactics, and we'll pick one winner for the rest of the campaign. Urgently this week: the knowledge website redesign needs concrete feedback on Joker's design and incorporation of Joseph's editorial elements; the social media agency selection must be expedited with a bidding session on July 15. Also critical: the Global Program Brochure clean version must go to Alan by July 14 for Dean Xiang's feedback, and the CKGSB-EuroCham EV event needs speaker invitations sent by July 15. Upcoming: the Global Marcom report draft is due for review by July 15, and the IE report translation (capped at 50% of the original) needs to kick off once Li Wei provides section recommendations.

meetings.title: Knowledge Website Redesign Review
meetings.summary: Team reviewed Joker's knowledge website redesign, noting it's similar to current site with menu improvements. They discussed combining elements from Joseph's more editorial-style design to show more articles and indices.

meetings.title: SEO and Program Prioritization Strategy
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to rank for AI, EVs, and smart manufacturing programs in China, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29, 2026
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on website menu, school brochure updates, CES quotes for social media, Ding Hight Hall video progress, Asia Start articles, Davos press release, and social media agency scouting. Concerns raised about Zhu Yang's ChatGPT-generated feedback and timeline for agency selection.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team plans a joint report between CKGSB and IE on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with op-eds and case studies, aiming for a September launch at events in Singapore.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Campaign Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed campaign results for Asia Start, planning for the new 'Intelligent China' program launching in December, and decided to test AI vs traditional strategies.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation & De-risking
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, de-risking constraints on companies, and the role of referendums in limiting population. Emphasis on government frameworks vs. company autonomy.

decisions.text: Remove dedicated video section; videos will be dispersed by topic.
Keep knowledge website redesign but incorporate more articles and indices from Joseph's design.
Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs; put US partnerships on hold initially.
Include Berkeley program as a third priority if resources allow.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, incorporating the 10 points or turning into a 10-point article.
Zhu Yang's feedback is not actionable; team will not spend excessive time on it.
IE report translation should not exceed 50% of the original Chinese report.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, with joint distribution.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Report will include a main research feature by Professor Leeway, plus op-eds and case studies.
Target launch at events in Singapore in mid-September (Tech in Asia Sep 15-16 or Tyson Sep 29-30).
For Asia Start first month, run two parallel routes: Joseph using AI, Daisy using traditional strategy, then decide on one approach for the rest.
